name: Ross Stores
cat: Retail / Off-Price Apparel and Home Fashion

political.s: Ross Stores made $42,018 in political contributions in 2024 with no lobbying activity or outside spending reported. The company maintains minimal federal political engagement focused on its business interests.

charity.s: Ross Stores Foundation focuses on youth education and development, having contributed over $60 million to Boys & Girls Clubs and supporting literacy programs. The company provides store-based gift cards and regional grants of $10,000-$30,000 to nonprofits serving socioeconomically disadvantaged youth.
charity.amt: Not disclosed (estimated $5-10 million annually across all programs)
charity.focus: Youth literacy, academic achievement, life skills development, disaster relief

environment.s: Ross achieved a B score on CDP 2024 Climate Change disclosure and targets 30% GHG reduction by 2025 from 2017 baseline. The company prioritizes energy efficiency, waste reduction, and approximately 88% of transportation partners participate in EPA SmartWay program; net-zero target set for 2050.

labor.s: Ross has incurred $17.8 million in regulatory penalties since 2000 and suppliers have faced wage violations. Violation Tracker identifies 34 records. Glassdoor DEI rating of 3.9/5 reflects average performance; workers report concerns about compensation and benefits compared to peers.

dei.s: Ross maintains an Office of Diversity, Equality and Inclusion with 46% of eligible associates in CommUnity Networks. The company publishes EEO-1 data and continues DEI commitments despite 2025 pressure to scale back; historically over $60 million contributed to support diverse communities.

animals.s: Ross does not seek products containing animal fur but acknowledges incidental fur may appear given opportunistic sourcing model. No specific animal testing policy published; products sourced from manufacturers after production.

guns.s: Ross Dress for Less is a retail apparel and home goods retailer with no connection to firearms sales or manufacturing. The company does not sell guns or ammunition.

privacy.s: Ross maintains privacy policies covering personal data protection using reasonable organizational and technical measures; however, the company acknowledges no system is 100% secure. Data may be transferred internationally and used for multiple purposes; third-party payment processors handle sensitive information.

execPay.s: CEO-to-worker pay ratio reached approximately 2,100:1 in 2023, among the highest in retail. New CEO Jim Conroy receives $47.47 million annually (99.5% bonuses/equity) while median employee earns ~$8,600; this represents significant disparity from industry average of 538:1.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
